board.category: THAT'S WHY WE CALL IT THAT

questions.clue: This keyboard layout was designed in part to slow typists down and reduce mechanical jams on early typewriters.
The term "spam" for unwanted messages comes from a sketch by this British comedy group.
The Pokémon creature Ekans gets its name from this animal.
This famous search engine began life under the much less catchy name "BackRub."
The Bluetooth logo combines two runes representing the initials of this Viking king.
"They're taking the hobbits to Isengard!" became an early viral remix featuring clips from this film trilogy.
This 2004 internet phenomenon featured a badly animated dancing baby peanut-butter enthusiast.
Before becoming a billionaire entrepreneur, this person was best known online for creating the website Facemash.
This phrase, originating from a 2014 Tumblr post, became shorthand for unexpectedly encountering something emotionally devastating online.
questions.answer: I was not emotionally prepared for this

questions.clue: This 1998 fan-created webpage is often cited as the oldest continuously updated webcomic on the internet.
This villain promised his nephew he'd 'always be there for him' before throwing his father off a cliff.
This Disney prince pretended to be in love with Anna to seize a kingdom.
This character sold out Neo and the crew of the Nebuchadnezzar in exchange for being reinserted into the Matrix.
In The Dark Knight, this district attorney became a villain after feeling betrayed by the system.
This advisor spent eight seasons manipulating rulers before finally being executed by Daenerys Targaryen.
This game has ruined friendships through four simple words: 'Do not pass Go.'
The unofficial slogan of this game is often 'It's basically friendship-ending Risk.'
This game asks players to convince everyone they are not secretly a fascist.
In this game, players race to connect cities using colored train cards.
This board game's title refers to a region of southern France and features tile placement as its core mechanic.

board.category: ARE YOU SURE YOU'RE A NEW ZEALANDER?

questions.clue: What colour are the stars on the New Zealand flag?
Which island is larger by area: the North Island or the South Island?
This town hosts the annual Burt Munro Challenge.
Before decimal currency was introduced in 1967, New Zealand used pounds, shillings, and this smaller unit.
Name either of the two New Zealand territories that use the New Zealand dollar but are not part of New Zealand proper.
"Mr. Stark, I don't feel so good."
"Always."
"You were my brother, Anakin! I loved you!"
"Tell Cersei. I want her to know it was me."
"My friends... you bow to no one."
The citizens of Pompeii were dealing with earthquakes before this volcano buried their city under ash.
This theme park's dinosaurs escaped their enclosures. Then the power went out.
This NASA mission suffered an oxygen tank explosion; then the crew had to use the lunar module as a lifeboat to survive.
In 1871, this city was already dealing with a drought when a fire broke out and destroyed much of the city.
During his invasion of Russia, his army captured Moscow, only for the city to burn and winter to arrive before they could secure victory.
A failed online dating website pivoted into what became the world's largest video-sharing platform.
Nintendo originally created this character as a carpenter before changing his profession.
The first version of this game was created because its developer wanted to make a cave-exploration game, not a global phenomenon.
This social media platform began as an internal communication tool for employees of a podcast company.
This company started as an online bookstore before expanding into almost everything else.
This singer's godmother is Dolly Parton.
Actors Chris, Liam, and Luke share this surname.
This actress from Mad Max: Fury Road and The Queen's Gambit is related to nobody famous at all.
Nicolas Cage changed his surname to avoid benefiting from his famous uncle, this director.
This actor, famous for playing Superman, is the cousin of actor Nicolas Cage.
This 2021 sequel revealed that a basketball movie starring Michael Jordan somehow needed a multiverse.
This 2023 shark movie answered the question nobody asked: 'What if there were even more giant sharks?'
This 2024 film was the sequel to a movie released 36 years earlier and once again featured a mischievous ghost.
This franchise somehow reached a tenth main installment despite starting as a film about street racing.
This franchise has released so many films that the titles now include words like 'Dominion' and 'Rebirth.'
Despite becoming an internet joke, this 2022 superhero film was re-released after fans jokingly demanded it.
This social media platform briefly changed its name to a single letter in 2023.
In 1992, this clear version of Coca-Cola's rival was released to great confusion and poor sales.
This movie studio released Cats in 2019 complete with unsettling digital fur technology.
This company released a ketchup-and-ranch combination called 'Kranch.'
This country's national animal is technically a unicorn.
Bananas are naturally slightly radioactive because they contain this element.
This bird can imitate chainsaws, camera shutters, car alarms, and human speech.
Octopuses have this many hearts.
This planet experiences rain made of diamonds, according to current scientific models.

finalJeopardy.clue: This element, discovered in 1868, was first identified in the Sun before it was found on Earth.
